name: Pre-Matric Scholarship — SC / ST / OBC / MBC
summary: State scholarship from the ST & SC Development Department for SC/ST/OBC students in Classes 9 and 10 — covers school costs, books, and uniform to help students stay enrolled.
income_limit: Family annual income ≤ ₹2.5 lakh/year (some components use lower limits — verify on portal)
deadline_pattern: Fresh applications typically open July–October; deadlines are frequently extended. Always check scholarship.odisha.gov.in for the current year's dates.
documents: Bank passbook (student's name, NPCI-seeded to Aadhaar for DBT)
notes: Aadhaar must be NPCI-mapped to bank account for DBT. A 14-digit NSP One-Time-Registration (OTR) number may be required for the 2026 cycle — verify on portal.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship (PRERANA) — SC / ST
summary: PRERANA — Odisha's main post-matric scholarship for SC and ST students who have passed Class 10 and are studying in Class 11, 12, ITI, diploma, or undergraduate courses.
income_limit: ₹2.5 lakh/year for SC/ST (some sources cite ₹8 lakh — strong inconsistency; verify on portal before quoting a figure)
deadline_pattern: Applications typically open August–October; renewals November–January; frequent extensions (e.g., extended to 6 March 2026 in a recent cycle). Verify on scholarship.odisha.gov.in.
documents: Hostel certificate (if hostel-based, for higher hosteller rate)
notes: Amounts range widely (₹15,000–₹1,20,000+/year) based on course group (Group I = professional/engineering/medical; Group IV = others) and hosteller vs day-scholar status. From AY 2020-21, applications moved from old PRERANA portal to the State Scholarship Portal. Freeship Card (separate entry) may also apply alongside this.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship (PRERANA) — OBC / SEBC / EBC
summary: PRERANA post-matric scholarship for OBC, SEBC, and EBC students in Class 11, 12, ITI, diploma, or undergraduate courses — covers tuition and maintenance costs.
income_limit: ₹1 lakh/year for OBC/SEBC in most sources (some cite higher limits — inconsistent; verify on portal before quoting)
deadline_pattern: Similar to SC/ST cycle: August–October opening, renewals November–January, frequent extensions. Check scholarship.odisha.gov.in.
notes: Income limit is inconsistent across sources — ₹1 lakh cited for OBC/SEBC vs ₹2.5 lakh vs ₹8 lakh in different documents. Do not quote a figure; direct student to the portal.

name: Sudakshya — Scholarship for Girls in Technical Education
odia_name: ସୁଦକ୍ଷ — ବାଳିକା ଛାତ୍ରବୃତ୍ତି (ITI / ପଲ୍ଲୀ ବିଜ୍ଞାନ ସଂସ୍ଥାନ)
summary: Odisha Skill Development Department scholarship exclusively for girls studying ITI, ITC, Polytechnic, or Diploma — pays monthly maintenance allowance, covers admission/tuition/exam fees, and provides apprenticeship support.
amount_inr: ₹1,500/month (hostellers) or ₹500/month (day scholars) + admission/tuition/exam fees covered + apprenticeship support: ₹1,000/month (in-state) or ₹1,500/month (out-of-state) + ₹5,000 one-time on getting employment outside Odisha after 6 months
deadline_pattern: Recent cycle extended to 21 January 2026. Verify current deadline on scholarship.odisha.gov.in.
notes: Open to ALL categories (not just SC/ST). Goal is to raise girls' share in technical education to ~30%. IMPORTANT: There appear to be two different 'Sudakshya' schemes on the portal — one for girls in technical education (this entry) and one for orphan/vulnerable children in higher education with an ₹8 lakh income limit. Verify which applies by reading the current scheme description on the portal.

name: Green Passage — Free Education for Orphan Students
odia_name: ସବୁଜ ପ୍ରବେଶ — ଅନାଥ ଛାତ୍ରଛାତ୍ରୀଙ୍କ ଶିକ୍ଷା ସହାୟତା
hindi_name: ग्रीन पैसेज — अनाथ छात्रों के लिए निःशुल्क शिक्षा
summary: Green Passage provides free education support for orphan students of all categories in Odisha — one of the few schemes explicitly targeted at students who have lost parents.
deadline_pattern: Verify current cycle dates on scholarship.odisha.gov.in.
documents: Death certificate(s) of parent(s) — proof of orphan status
Income certificate / BPL card if required (check portal)

name: Krishi Vidya Nidhi Yojana (KALIA Scholarship) — Farmers' Children
summary: Agriculture Department scholarship for children of CM-KISAN / KALIA scheme beneficiary farmers — supports professional and technical courses including engineering, medical, agriculture, nursing, and ITI.
deadline_pattern: Verify on scholarship.odisha.gov.in. Parent must be a verified CM-KISAN beneficiary — confirm this status first.
documents: CM-KISAN / KALIA beneficiary certificate of parent/guardian
notes: Eligibility gate: the parent/guardian must be a verified CM-KISAN (or KALIA predecessor) beneficiary. Check with the local Agriculture office or CM-KISAN portal if unsure.

name: Nirman Shramik Kalyan Yojana (NSKY) — Children of Construction Workers
odia_name: ନିର୍ମାଣ ଶ୍ରମିକ କଲ୍ୟାଣ ଯୋଜନା (NSKY) — ଶ୍ରମିକ ପୁଅ-ଝିଅ
hindi_name: निर्माण श्रमिक कल्याण योजना (NSKY) — निर्माण मजदूर के बच्चे
summary: Education assistance for children of workers registered under the Odisha Building & Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board (OB&OCWWB) — a key anti-dropout tool for construction-labour families.
deadline_pattern: A recent cycle deadline was extended to 31 July 2026. Verify current dates on scholarship.odisha.gov.in.
documents: OB&OCWWB registration card of parent/guardian (parent must be a registered construction worker)
notes: Eligibility gate: parent/guardian must be registered with the Odisha Building & Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board. If not registered, help them register first. This is the single most targeted dropout-prevention lever for children of construction/daily-labour families.

name: Freeship Card — ST / SC Students (Fee Admission Without Prepayment)
summary: A government declaration of scholarship eligibility that allows ST/SC students to be admitted without paying fees upfront — once issued it is valid for the entire course duration, removing the biggest immediate financial barrier to admission.
amount_inr: Fee waiver at the point of admission (not a cash amount)
deadline_pattern: Applied for at the time of admission. Inquire with the institution's scholarship nodal officer before paying any admission fees.
notes: Critical for students who cannot pay fees upfront at the time of college/ITI/polytechnic admission. The student must mention Freeship Card eligibility to the institution before paying — most institutions will hold the seat without payment once eligibility is confirmed.

name: Chhatra Protsahan Yojana (CPY) — Free JEE / NEET / AIIMS Coaching for ST / SC
odia_name: ଛାତ୍ର ପ୍ରୋତ୍ସାହନ ଯୋଜନା (CPY) — ମାଗଣା JEE/NEET ତାଲିମ
hindi_name: छात्र प्रोत्साहन योजना (CPY) — निःशुल्क JEE/NEET कोचिंग
summary: Free residential JEE / NEET / AIIMS coaching at Centres of Excellence run by Odisha's ST & SC Development Department — about 320 ST/SC students selected per year across roughly 7 centres.
deadline_pattern: Selection process typically announced before Class 11/12. Verify current dates and centre locations with the ST & SC Development Department, Odisha (stsc.odisha.gov.in).
documents: Class 10 marksheet (for Class 11 entry) or Class 11/12 marks (for Class 12 entry)
notes: Launched 4 October 2021. Most available data is from launch — verify current scale, number of centres, and seat count with the department. IMPORTANT: This is NOT the 'Akanksha' or 'Super-30' coaching scheme — those are Madhya Pradesh and Tripura schemes respectively and do not apply to Odisha students.

name: Central Free Coaching Scheme — SC / OBC (Ministry of Social Justice)
summary: Central government scheme from the Ministry of Social Justice providing free coaching for SC and OBC students for competitive exams (UPSC, SSC, banking, engineering, medical) — about 3,500 seats per year nationwide with a monthly stipend.
amount_inr: Full coaching fee covered + ~₹4,000/month stipend during coaching
deadline_pattern: Verify on coaching.dosje.gov.in — seats fill fast. Applications typically open April–June.
documents: Income certificate (family income below ₹8 lakh/year — verify current limit on portal)

name: PM POSHAN Mid-Day Meal — Extended to Classes 9 & 10 in Odisha
odia_name: PM POSHAN ମଧ୍ୟାହ୍ନ ଭୋଜନ — ଶ୍ରେଣୀ ୯ ଓ ୧୦ (ଓଡ଼ିଶା)
hindi_name: PM POSHAN मध्याह्न भोजन — कक्षा 9 और 10 (ओडिशा)
summary: Odisha extended the PM POSHAN (Mid-Day Meal) free cooked lunch to Class 9 and 10 students in government and aided schools — announced February 2025 specifically to reduce secondary-level dropout rates.
amount_inr: Free cooked meal each school day
deadline_pattern: No application needed — automatic for students enrolled in eligible schools. Only available in government, government-aided, and local-body schools (NOT private unaided schools).
notes: Announced by CM Mohan Charan Majhi on 11 February 2025 at Ranihat High School, Cuttack centenary. The national scheme covers only up to Class 8 — Odisha's extension to Class 9–10 is a state-specific addition. Meals cooked by Mission Shakti women's SHGs. Also announced alongside: Sahid Madho Singh Hata Kharcha Yojana (pocket expense support — verify separately on portal).
